frameworks.name: neural plasticity
frameworks.one_line: tDCS modulates task-specific plasticity during cognitive training.

frameworks.name: transfer of learning
frameworks.one_line: Whether training gains generalize to untrained cognitive tasks.

core_question: Does tDCS stimulation intensity modulate cognitive training gains and their transfer to untrained tasks?
key_assumptions: tDCS modulates cortical excitability in a dose-dependent manner
Multitasking training engages plasticity mechanisms susceptible to stimulation
manipulations: tDCS dosage at three levels (0.7, 1.0, 2.0 mA) during multitasking training
key_findings: Higher tDCS dosage produced larger training gains on the trained task
Higher dosage did not produce significantly greater transfer to untrained tasks
interpretations: Stimulation dose modulates plasticity for the trained task specifically
Dose does not broaden generalization across cognitive domains
